weatherForecast.comment: A cold front will bring heavy cloud until late morning, poor visibility, light snowfall. In afternoon hours clouds will disperse from the west, towards evening skies will clear completely. Temperature at 2000m: -3 degrees; at 3000m: -9 degrees. Moderate southwesterly-to-northwesterly winds at high altitudes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Beware being swept along by snowdrift slides!
avalancheActivity.comment: Main danger: sliding away and falling atop hard, smooth surfaces and being swept along by small-sized snowdrift slides. Outside of that, during the course of the day, small-sized loose-snow avalanches can trigger naturally in steep rocky terrain.

snowpackStructure.comment: Regionally approximately 10cm of fresh snow is anticipated, somewhat more in Allgau. On sunny slopes it will be deposited on a hardly frozen surface, on shady slopes often atop surface hoar and a rain crust. No further triggerings in older weak layers of the snowpack have been registered in recent days at the Avalanche Warning Service.

tendency.highlights: On Friday no change expected, as the result of an intermediate high-pressure front.

weatherForecast.comment: A cold front will bring heavy cloud until late morning, poor visibility, light snowfall. In afternoon hours clouds will disperse from the west, towards evening skies will clear completely. Temperature at 2000m: -3 degrees; at 3000m: -9 degrees. Moderate southwesterly-to-northwesterly winds at high altitudes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Generally favorable conditions. Caution urged on steep shady slopes.
avalancheActivity.comment: Isolated avalanches can still be triggered in the weak old snow by winter sports enthusiasts, particularly in little-skied backcountry terrain where the surface is not capable of bearing loads. Danger zones occur especially in spots where the snow is relatively shallow, and in transitions from shallow to deep snow. Avalanches can trigger down to deeper layers inside the snowpack and grow to large size. A cautious route selection and maintaining safety distances between tourers on ascents and descents are recommended. On hard-frozen steep slopes, the danger of sliding and falling require special caution.

snowpackStructure.comment: Regionally approximately 10cm of fresh snow is anticipated, on sunny slopes deposited atop a hardly frozen surface, on shady slopes frequently atop surface hoar and below 2300-2500m atop a rain crust. The middle layer of the snowpack often consists of settled, well consolidated layers which on shady slopes at higher altitudes blanket at old snow fundament with large sized cup-shaped crystals.

tendency.highlights: On Friday no change expected, as the result of an intermediate high-pressure front.

ALBINA.generalHeadline: Caution urged on steep shady slopes.
